P1_Criteria: Critical signals requiring immediate attention: CEO/CFO/C-suite leadership changes, major stock drops (>10%), bankruptcy filings, hostile takeovers, major lawsuits or regulatory actions, product recalls, or severe reputational crises.
P2_Criteria: Significant business events to monitor closely: IPOs, major funding rounds (Series B+), strategic partnerships, mergers & acquisitions, significant market expansion, large contract wins, or notable competitor moves.
P3_Criteria: Routine informational news: minor product updates, general industry news, small funding announcements (Seed/Series A), routine earnings reports, awards/recognition, or general company announcements with low urgency.
P4_Criteria: Market intelligence and growth opportunities: new product launches, technology innovations, R&D initiatives, patent filings, industry conference participation, customer success stories, market share changes, expansion into new verticals, hiring trends (especially key roles like Chief Revenue Officer, VP Sales), digital transformation initiatives, or sustainability/ESG commitments that indicate strategic direction changes.
P5_Criteria: Competitive and ecosystem insights: competitor analysis relevant to the client, supply chain developments, industry regulatory changes affecting their sector, technology adoption trends, customer sentiment shifts, analyst ratings or coverage, social media buzz or viral mentions, thought leadership content (blogs, whitepapers), participation in industry associations, or any contextual market research that provides conversation starters for lead conversion discussions.
